fields.name: Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a cause of absence code.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description for the cause of absence.

fields.name: Unit of Measure Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

fields.name: Total Absence (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total number of absences (calculated in days or hours) for all employees.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a filter for a dimension. If the field contains a dimension filter, the figure in the Total Absence (Base) field will be based only on the dimension values included in the filter.
To see the dimension values you have set up for a dimension in the Dimension Value table, click the field.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a filter for a dimension. If the field contains a dimension filter, the figure in the Total Absence (Base) field will be based only on the dimension values included in the filter.
To see the dimension values you have set up for a dimension in the Dimension Value table, click the field.

fields.name: Employee No. Filter
fields.tooltip:
If the field contains an Employee No. Filter, the figure in the Total Absence (Base) field will be based only on the employee numbers included in the filter.
You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters. There are certain rules for how these can be combined:

Meaning
Sample Expression
Included

Equal to

1

Data linked to Employee 1 (the employee number could contain letters instead of just numbers).


Interval

1..5

Data linked to Employees 1 through 5.


Either/or

1|2

Data linked to Employees 1 or 2.


Different from

<>1

Data linked to all Employees except Employee 1.


To see the employee numbers in the Employee List window, click the field.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval. For example, the Total Absence (Base) field shows a total for that interval only.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.
